name: Texas Resolution Group
description_short: Texas Resolution Group is a debt resolution company offering virtual, attorney-assisted negotiation between debtors and creditors to settle outstanding debts without court proceedings.
description_long:
Texas Resolution Group, LLC is a Texas-based debt relief company founded with the mission to help individuals and businesses resolve debt issues efficiently and affordably. The company has been operating for 15 years and claims to have assisted over 10,000 people across Texas with a reported success rate exceeding 99%. They position themselves as an alternative to traditional collection agencies and court-based debt resolution processes.

The company offers virtual debt resolution services that facilitate negotiation between clients, creditors, and debt collectors. Their core service uses an attorney-assisted process that eliminates the need for in-person meetings between parties. Instead, Texas Resolution Group acts as an intermediary, communicating back and forth between clients and creditors to reach mutually acceptable settlements. They work with major banks (Bank of America, Chase, Discover, American Express, Citibank, Capital One, Wells Fargo), debt purchasing companies, collection agencies, and other creditors.

Texas Resolution Group differentiates itself through their virtual-first approach, claiming this saves time and money while reducing the discomfort of face-to-face confrontations. Their process allows parties to deliberate positions carefully without meeting-table pressure and eliminates scheduling delays. The company emphasizes cost efficiency, integrity, and personable communication with all parties involved.

While the company presents a legitimate debt resolution alternative, consumers should note that specific fee structures, success rate documentation, and attorney credentials are not detailed on the website. The claims of 99% success rate and 15-year track record are stated but not independently verified. Potential clients should inquire about fee transparency, state licensing, and what specific settlements typically look like before engaging services.
pros: Virtual process eliminates need for physical meetings, saving time and travel costs for clients
Attorney-assisted negotiation model provides legal guidance throughout the resolution process
Claims to work with major creditors (Bank of America, Chase, American Express, Wells Fargo, Discover) suggesting established industry relationships
Removes pressure of face-to-face negotiations by allowing parties time to deliberate positions carefully
Stated 15-year track record in Texas debt resolution market with claim of 99% success rate
Non-adversarial approach positioned as alternative to expensive litigation and collection processes
Can commence quickly without scheduling delays typical of formal legal proceedings
cons: Website provides no specific fee structure, pricing models, or cost transparency for potential clients
Success rate of 99% is claimed but not independently verified or defined (unclear what 'success' means)
No information about attorney credentials, licensing, bar membership, or qualifications
Limited detail on what services are actually included or how the virtual negotiation process technically works
No client testimonials, case studies, or third-party reviews visible on the website
best_for: Texas residents with multiple debts in collections seeking settlement without litigation
Individuals who are geographically distant from creditors and prefer to avoid in-person meetings
People overwhelmed by debt collection calls and legal notices who need guided negotiation assistance
Those seeking an alternative to expensive bankruptcy or formal legal proceedings
diagnosis: Texas Resolution Group is best suited for Texas residents with debts in collections who want attorney-assisted settlement without court proceedings and prefer virtual communication. The main caveat is that the website lacks transparent fee information, independent verification of claims, and details about attorney qualifications—prospective clients should request clear pricing and credentials before engaging.
services: Virtual debt resolution and settlement negotiation
Attorney-assisted debt communication between clients and creditors
Court system debt resolution for cases already in litigation
Creditor negotiation on behalf of clients
Ongoing communication toggling between parties until resolution reached
Guidance through debt issues and available options
